issues.comment: Constructor parameter type `InsightsBookingServicePublicOptions` allows optional `teamId` but validation with `insightsBookingServiceOptionsSchema` requires `teamId` for team scope, causing runtime errors when TypeScript types suggest it should work.
Removed caching tests for authorization and filter conditions means caching behavior is no longer verified, potentially missing bugs in the caching implementation that still exists in the service.

SQL template `ANY(${teamIds})` with potentially empty `teamIds` array may cause unexpected behavior - while PostgreSQL handles empty arrays, it would match no rows which might not be intended if there are no teams to check.

Test uses `ANY(${[testData.user.id]})` with single-element array which works in PostgreSQL but the array construction `[testData.user.id,]` has trailing comma that could indicate copy-paste error or inconsistent formatting.
